name: Lemon Pudding Cake from Moosewood
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 cup sugar, or more to taste
1/2 cup fresh lemon juice, about 4 lemons
recipeInstructions:
1. Beat 2 egg yolks lightly.
2. Add 2 teaspoons lemon zest, optional.
3. Beat 4 egg whites until stiff.
4. Add a pinch of salt to the egg whites.
5. In a large bowl, combine flour, baking powder, and 3/4 cup sugar.
- Use whole cup sugar for sweeter confection.
6. Stir in buttermilk, lemon juice, beaten egg yolks, and grated lemon zest (if using).
7. With an electric mixer, beat the remaining 1/2 cup sugar into the egg whites until stiff.
8. Gently fold the egg white mixture into the batter.
9. Spoon the batter evenly into prepared ramekins or dessert cups.
10. Pour boiling water into a baking pan so it reaches halfway up the sides of the cups.
11. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
12. Bake for 50 to 55 minutes, until puffy and golden.
Nutritional Information per serving:
- 229 Calories
- 6.0g Protein
- 2.6g Fat
- 46.6g Carbohydrates
- 0.9g Saturated Fatty Acids
- 0.3g Unsaturated Fatty Acids
- 0.9g Monounsaturated Fatty Acids
- 87mg Cholesterol
- 139mg Sodium
- 0.1g Dietary Fiber
Notes:
This favorite old-fashioned dessert separates into layers during baking—a tart-sweet pudding on the bottom and a light spongy cake on top. Serve it warm or chilled, plain or with fresh berries, for picnics, potlucks, family meals, or special dinners. Tightly sealed and refrigerated, Lemon Pudding Cake will keep for about 5 days.
